item.description: According to two U.S. officials, the Justice Department is pushing to indict former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the deaths of four Cuban Americans in the 1996 downing of civilian planes. NBC News' Ryan Reilly breaks down the state of relations between the U.S. and Cuba.

media:content.media:title: DOJ pushing to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 downing of civilian planes
media:content.media:description: According to two U.S. officials, the Justice Department is pushing to indict former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the deaths of four Cuban Americans in the 1996 downing of civilian planes. NBC News' Ryan Reilly breaks down the state of relations between the U.S. and Cuba.

item.title: DOJ pushing to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 downing of civilian planes

item.description: A man suspected of plotting a terror attack on a synagogue in New York City has been arrested. NBC News' Tom Winter has details on the arrest and the suspect's alleged involvement with a group tied to Hezbollah.

media:content.media:title: Man accused of plotting terror attack on New York City synagogue arrested
media:content.media:description: A man suspected of plotting a terror attack on a synagogue in New York City has been arrested. NBC News' Tom Winter has details on the arrest and the suspect's alleged involvement with a group tied to Hezbollah.

item.title: Man accused of plotting terror attack on New York City synagogue arrested

item.description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.

media:content.media:title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.

item.title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?

item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas
Dawn Liu
Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Beijing has put the robotics industry front and center of its national agenda as the tech race with Washington heats up in several key areas, including AI.

media:content.media:text: X-Humanoid in Beijing.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: A production line at X-Humanoid in Beijing.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: The X-Humanoid robotics lab, top, and humanoids on display, below.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

item.title: Inside China’s race to dominate humanoid robotics industry

item.dc:creator: Janis Mackey Frayer
Dawn Liu
Jay Ganglani
item.description: They’ve never met, but Ping Ping and Fu Shuang are getting ready for the blind date of a lifetime.

media:content.media:text: Ping Ping in Chengdu, China in May 2026.
media:content.media:description: Ping Ping in Chengdu, China in May 2026.

media:content.media:text: Fu Shuang in Chengdu, China, in early May.
media:content.media:description: Fu Shuang in Chengdu, China, in early May.

item.title: Pandas set for 8,000-mile blind date in Atlanta

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason stands on a set of stairs inside of his home
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A four image composite: top left image shows a pill being poured out of a pill bottle onto a hand, top right shows Melissa helping Jason put on a shirt, bottom left show Melissa preparing food on a countertop, and bottom right shows Melissa shaving Jason's face
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce wheels her son Jason, in a wheelchair, on a sidewalk outside in a residential area
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce's hands are seen holding one of Jason's hands, there is a bump on the knuckle of his middle finger
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A large crowd of people hold signs against medicaid cuts outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds up a cellphone displaying a photo of Jason Gonce seated in a baby chair as a baby
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A three image composite: the image on the left shows two black t-shirt's for Casa Mia's restaurant hanging on a hanger on a door, middle image shows Melissa embracing Jason, right image shows bills and coupons on a countertop
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Michele Gregory and her partner and son (in a wheelchair) pose for a family portrait outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Casey Barrett and his son hug his daughter, center, who is seated in a wheelchair, she holds two teddy bears, they are all in a field
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Anna Keyzer and family members pose by a tree on a sidewalk for a family portrait
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce shaves Jason's chin in their bathroom
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason, left, playing with a blue toy, sits next to his brother,
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce seated at her kitchen island
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce and her two sons walk down the stairs, seen from their backs
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

item.title: Families caring for disabled relatives face unthinkable choices as Medicaid cuts loom

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: The Trump administration’s moves to make flavored vapes more widely available have caused a splintering among some in the MAHA movement and some federal health officials.

media:content.media:text: FDA Commissioner Martin Makary speaks alongside President Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary speaks alongside President Donald Trump during a press conference at the White House in 2025.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Signs Executive Order Reclassifying Marijuana As Low Risk Drug
media:content.media:description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary speaks alongside President Donald Trump during a press conference at the White House in 2025.

item.title: Trump’s flavored vape push sparks backlash from some MAHA influencers and administration officials

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: A Michigan State student appeared to be brandishing a knife and running toward officers when he was shot in multiple waves of police gunfire, according to body camera video of the deadly confrontation released Friday

media:content.media:text: Karyn Kirby, whose son, Isaiah Kirby, was shot and killed by police in East Lansing
media:content.media:description: Karyn Kirby, whose son, Isaiah Kirby, was shot and killed by police in April, speaks in Lansing, Mich., on May 12.

media:content.media:text: Three police officers face away from the camera and toward a man lying on the ground in front of them.
media:content.media:description: Karyn Kirby, whose son, Isaiah Kirby, was shot and killed by police in April, speaks in Lansing, Mich., on May 12.

item.title: Police release 911 calls and video connected to fatal shooting of Michigan State student

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
item.description: Democratic Gov. Jared Polis had previously called Peters’ 9-year prison sentence “harsh.” President Trump has repeatedly demanded she be released.

media:content.media:text: Former Mesa County clerk Tina Peters listens during her trial on March 3, 2023, in Grand Junction, Colorado.
media:content.media:description: Former Mesa County clerk Tina Peters during her trial in Grand Junction, Colo., in 2023.

media:content.media:text: Colorado Governor Jared Polis speaks in Boulder, Colorado on June 4, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Former Mesa County clerk Tina Peters during her trial in Grand Junction, Colo., in 2023.

item.title: Election denier Tina Peters set for release after Colorado governor commutes sentence

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Kristen Welker wraps up President Donald Trump’s trip to China. Plus, we look ahead to next Tuesday’s key Republican Senate primary in Georgia.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Trump Meets With China's President Xi And Attends State Banquet
media:content.media:description: China's President Xi Jinping (L) and US President Donald Trump visit the Temple of Heaven on May 14, 2026 in Beijing, China.

item.title: Trump’s China trips ends with more questions than answers: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Laura Strickler
item.description: The town of Social Circle, Georgia, alleges in a lawsuit that ICE’s new facility will overburden the town’s small infrastructure resulting in “dry taps and raw human waste spills.”

media:content.media:text: Social Circle warehouse.
media:content.media:description: DHS purchased this warehouse in Social Circle, Ga., to use as a detention facility.

item.title: Atlanta suburb sues DHS over planned ICE facility that could hold 10,000 people

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: California’s spate of mushroom poisonings, which has killed four people and hospitalized 43 others, is the largest known outbreak of its kind in U.S. history, experts say.

media:content.media:text: Death cap mushrooms.
media:content.media:description: One square centimeter of a death cap mushroom, also called Amanita phalloides, can contain enough toxin to be fatal.

media:content.media:text: A Death Cap mushroom.
media:content.media:description: One square centimeter of a death cap mushroom, also called Amanita phalloides, can contain enough toxin to be fatal.

item.title: California’s growing mushroom poisoning outbreak is the biggest-ever in U.S.

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: Convicted double murderer and diagnosed sexual sadist Mitchell Gaff will likely be chewing on this for the rest of his life — in prison

media:content.media:text: Mitchell Gaff during his sentencing in Everett, Wash., on May 13, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Mitchell Gaff during his sentencing Wednesday in Everett, Wash.

media:content.media:text: Judith Weaver.
media:content.media:description: Mitchell Gaff during his sentencing Wednesday in Everett, Wash.

media:content.media:text: Susan Veasey.
media:content.media:description: Mitchell Gaff during his sentencing Wednesday in Everett, Wash.

item.title: How cops used chewing gum to make a breakthrough in two cold case murders

item.dc:creator: Steve Kornacki
Doha Madani
Joe Prince-Wright, NBC Sports
item.description: The 151st Preakness will be run tomorrow at Laurel Park in Maryland.

media:content.media:text: A tight group of racehorses and jockeys race toward the camera on a dirt track.
media:content.media:description: The field heads into the first turn during the 150th Preakness Stakes at Pimlico Race Course in Baltimore, Md. on May 17, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Son Heung-min.
media:content.media:description: The field heads into the first turn during the 150th Preakness Stakes at Pimlico Race Course in Baltimore, Md. on May 17, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Bukayo Saka.
media:content.media:description: The field heads into the first turn during the 150th Preakness Stakes at Pimlico Race Course in Baltimore, Md. on May 17, 2025.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Preakness preview

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Scott Wong
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson weighed in on domestic abuse allegations Rep. Max Miller’s ex-wife Emily Moreno has made in their divorce, saying “he’s got to work that out.”

media:content.media:text: Max Miller standing with his hands in his pockets in a Capitol hallway.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Max Miller, R-Ohio, at the U.S. Capitol in 2023.

item.title: Speaker Mike Johnson responds to domestic abuse allegations against a GOP congressman: ‘He’s got to work that out’

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Dienst
Tom Winter
Chloe Atkins
Matt Lavietes
item.description: A terrorist commander was accused of planning three attacks across the U.S. and faced terrorism charges on Wednesday

media:content.media:text: Mohammad al-Saadi in custody.
media:content.media:description: Mohammad al-Saadi in custody.

item.title: Alleged terrorist commander accused of planning attacks in NYC, L.A. and Arizona

item.dc:creator: Katherine Doyle
item.description: Though U.S. President Donald Trump said he and Chinese President Xi Jinping “discussed almost everything,” their Beijing summit produced no sweeping agreements.

media:content.media:text: CHINA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump leaves China with no agreement on thorny issues, but cites ‘very good’ talks with Xi

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis’ attempt to do away with anti-gerrymandering provisions in the state constitution had its first day in court Friday, as opposing attorneys pushed a Tallahassee judge to put a temporary hold on new congressional lines that created four additional GOP-leaning seats.

media:content.media:text: Oliver Larkin, Democratic candidate for Congress in Florida’s 23rd district, speaks during an emergency town hall that he held to address Florida Republicans’ newly approved congressional redistricting map (seen on wall).
media:content.media:description: Oliver Larkin, Democratic candidate for Congress in Florida’s 23rd District, speaks during an emergency town hall that he held to address Florida Republicans’ newly approved congressional redistricting map on May 4 in Coral Springs.

item.title: Court fight over Ron DeSantis’ new congressional map kicks off in Florida

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Brennan Leach
item.description: With his time in the governor’s office coming to an end, Brian Kemp has asked Georgia voters for “one last thing.”“Send Derek Dooley to the U.S.

media:content.media:text: State of the State Georgia
media:content.media:description: Gov. Brian Kemp delivers the State of the State address in Atlanta on Jan. 15.

item.title: Georgia Senate race tests Gov. Brian Kemp’s sway in the Republican Party

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Rep. Steve Cohen, D-Tenn., announced Friday that he won’t be running for re-election this fall after a redistricting push in Tennessee carved up his Memphis-based district.“This morning, I announced my decision not to run in any of the three gerrymandered congressional districts carved out of the 9th District that I have represented for more than 19 years,” Cohen said in a statement.“Last week Tennessee Republicans silenced the Black vote here in Memphis to make Republican victories like

media:content.media:text: Rep. Steve Cohen Holds News Conference On Punishment For Marijuana Offenses
media:content.media:description: Rep. Steve Cohen, D-Tenn., after testifying last week during a special session of the state Legislature to redraw U.S. congressional maps.

item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen to retire after Republican-led redistricting in Tennessee

item.dc:creator: Adam Reiss
Daniel Arkin
item.description: The judge in former Hollywood mogul Harvey Weinstein’s third New York sex crimes case declared a mistrial Friday after the jury said it was deadlocked on a verdict

media:content.media:text: Harvey Weinstein sits at a table in court
media:content.media:description: Former Hollywood film producer Harvey Weinstein appears in Manhattan Supreme Court during his retrial on April 14, 2026 in New York.

item.title: Harvey Weinstein’s third Manhattan sex crimes trial ruled mistrial as jury deadlocks

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: I found the best way to get rid of mosquitoes after talking with pest control experts. They recommend the best products and methods for making your home mosquito-free.

media:content.media:text: Experts recommend using products like mosquito dunks and plug-in traps to get rid of mosquitoes.
media:content.media:description: Experts recommend using products like mosquito dunks and plug-in traps to get rid of mosquitoes.

media:content.media:text: Experts recommend using products like mosquito dunks and plug-in traps to get rid of mosquitoes.
media:content.media:description: Experts recommend using products like mosquito dunks and plug-in traps to get rid of mosquitoes.

item.title: How to get rid of mosquitoes at home and keep them out

item.dc:creator: Bianca Alvarez
item.description: The best under-eye patches are designed to combat under-eye concerns. Shop nourishing options from Topicals, Grace & Stella, Dieux and more.

media:content.media:text: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Emma Satin, NBC Page
media:content.media:text: Grace & Stella's under-eye patches come in three different formulations: energizing, illuminating and moisturizing.
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

media:content.media:text: Ashley Morris wearing the blue Summer Fridays Jet Lag Eye Patches, in a white robe against a white, tiled bathroom wall.
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

media:content.media:text: Woman wearing pink under-eye patches
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

media:content.media:text: Godio uses these Peace Out patches on mornings when her eyes feel and look puffy and tired.
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

media:content.media:text: Woman applying patches under her eyes
media:content.media:description: The masks are shaped to fit in the under-eye area and come infused with ingredients like retinol, vitamin C and hyaluronic acid to nourish skin.

item.title: We tested 50+ under eye patches — here are the best ones for dark circles, dry skin and more

item.dc:creator: Ashley Morris
item.description: Dermatologists and a tattoo artist share their favorite sunscreens for tattoos to protect against UV damage from EltamD, Supergoop and more.

media:content.media:text: Sunscreen can help prevent tattoos from fading, distortion, sunburn and infection, according to experts.
media:content.media:description: Sunscreen can help prevent tattoos from fading, distortion, sunburn and infection, according to experts.

media:content.media:text: Sunscreen can help prevent tattoos from fading, distortion, sunburn and infection, according to experts.
media:content.media:description: Sunscreen can help prevent tattoos from fading, distortion, sunburn and infection, according to experts.

item.title: What does the sun do to your tattoos? We asked experts to find out
